_comment: Heuristics for flagging an application's federated identity credential (Workload Identity Federation) as overly broad. A federated credential lets an external OIDC identity (e.g. a GitHub Actions workflow) authenticate AS the application with no client secret, inheriting all of the app's permissions. If the trusted subject is broad (wildcard, any pull request, or not pinned to a specific branch/environment), anyone who can run in that external context can impersonate the app. This is a curated heuristic, not an authoritative rule; tune the lists below for your CI/CD setup.
